title: St. Jean Braves

infobox.value: 45-53 | 4th | [Don Savage](/wiki/Don_Savage "Don Savage") / [Red Hayworth](/wiki/Red_Hayworth "Red Hayworth") | No playoffs

(French: *Les Canadiens de Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu*) were a [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") team which existed between 1948 and 1955.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Based in [Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu](/wiki/Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu "Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu"), [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec") the club competed in the [Provincial League](/wiki/Provincial_League_(baseball) "Provincial League (baseball)"). The franchise was founded in 1948 as the **Saint-Jean Braves**.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It won the league title in 1950, and was a class-C affiliate of the [Pittsburgh Pirates](/wiki/Pittsburgh_Pirates "Pittsburgh Pirates") from 1952 to 1955.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

(vs. [Sherbrooke Athlétiques](/w/index.php?title=Sherbrooke_Athl%C3%A9tiques&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sherbrooke Athlétiques (page does not exist)"), 4 games to 3) Won in 1st round (vs. [Farnham Pirates](/wiki/Farnham_Pirates "Farnham Pirates"), 4 games to 1) |

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [St. Jean Braves](/w/index.php?title=St._Jean_Braves&redirect=no "St. Jean Braves"))

Minor league baseball team

| Saint-Jean Canadiens | |
| --- | --- |
| * **[1948](/wiki/1948_in_baseball "1948 in baseball")***–***[1955](/wiki/1955_in_baseball "1955 in baseball")** * **[Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu, Quebec](/wiki/Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu,_Quebec "Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu, Quebec")** | |
| Minor league affiliations | |
| Previous classes | * Class C (1950-1955) * Independent (1948-1949) |
| Previous leagues | [Provincial League](/wiki/Provincial_League_(baseball) "Provincial League (baseball)") |
| Major league affiliations | |
| Previous teams | * [Pittsburgh Pirates](/wiki/Pittsburgh_Pirates "Pittsburgh Pirates") (1952-1955) |
| Minor league titles | |
| League titles (1) | * 1950 |
| Team data | |
| Previous names | * Saint-Jean Canadians (1952-1955) * Saint-Jean Braves (1948-1951) |

The **Saint-Jean Canadiens** (French: *Les Canadiens de Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu*) were a [minor league baseball](/wiki/Minor_league_baseball "Minor league baseball") team which existed between 1948 and 1955.[[1]](#cite_note-1) Based in [Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu](/wiki/Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu "Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu"), [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec") the club competed in the [Provincial League](/wiki/Provincial_League_(baseball) "Provincial League (baseball)"). The franchise was founded in 1948 as the **Saint-Jean Braves**.[[2]](#cite_note-2) It won the league title in 1950, and was a class-C affiliate of the [Pittsburgh Pirates](/wiki/Pittsburgh_Pirates "Pittsburgh Pirates") from 1952 to 1955.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Season-by-season

| Year | Record | Finish | Manager | Playoffs |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1948 | 56-44 | 2nd | [Jean-Pierre Roy](/wiki/Jean-Pierre_Roy "Jean-Pierre Roy") / [Bobby Estalella](/wiki/Bobby_Estalella_(outfielder) "Bobby Estalella (outfielder)") | No playoffs |
| 1949 | 45-53 | 4th | [Don Savage](/wiki/Don_Savage "Don Savage") / [Red Hayworth](/wiki/Red_Hayworth "Red Hayworth") | No playoffs |
| 1950 | 58-49 | 1st | Steve Mizerak | **League Champs** (vs. [Sherbrooke Athlétiques](/w/index.php?title=Sherbrooke_Athl%C3%A9tiques&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sherbrooke Athlétiques (page does not exist)"), 4 games to 3) Won in 1st round (vs. [Farnham Pirates](/wiki/Farnham_Pirates "Farnham Pirates"), 4 games to 1) |
| 1951 | 52-68 | 6th | Steve Mizerak |  |
| 1952 | 63-66 | 3rd | [Gordon Maltzberger](/wiki/Gordon_Maltzberger "Gordon Maltzberger") | Lost in 1st round (vs. [St. Hyacinthe A's](/wiki/St._Hyacinthe_A%27s "St. Hyacinthe A's"), 4 games to 3) |
| 1953 | 57-65 | 6th | [George Genovese](/wiki/George_Genovese "George Genovese") |  |
| 1954 | 53-73 | 5th | [George DeTore](/wiki/George_DeTore "George DeTore") / Steve Mizerak |  |
| 1955 | 86-44 | 1st | Steve Mizerak / Fred Luciano | Lost in 1st round (vs. [Burlington A's](/wiki/Burlington_A%27s "Burlington A's"), 4 games to 1) |
